reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by sending valid DataColumnSidecars, causing the node's ReconstructDataColumnSidecars function to randomly order cell indices due to Go map iteration (lines 79-82), which violates the c-kzg-4844 library requirement for sorted indices, breaking PeerDAS reconstruction. All gates passed: function has multiple non-test callers (sync/execution components), entry point is untrusted P2P, and PeerDAS is in-scope per EIP-7594.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: EIP-7594: Recovery cell_indices must be unique and sorted in ascending order
